location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in-office; Wednesday optional WFH; Friday depends on role/team

level_signals: Comp range $202K-$230K base consistent with Senior IC at NYC-based tech company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience, ideally focusing on search, retrieval, AI, or platform products.
must_haves.qualifier: ideally focusing on search, retrieval, AI, or platform products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong technical competency in translating customer needs and technical constraints into a multi-year platform thesis.
Proven ability to work with engineering teams on complex, ambiguous systems like APIs, automation, or agents.
Demonstrated curiosity about AI tools and emerging technologies, with a willingness to learn and leverage them to enhance productivity, collaboration, or decision-making.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to influence cross-functional partners and leadership.
Comfortable with data, metrics, and experimentation to drive product judgment.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with LLMs, RAG, search quality, or evaluation systems is a plus.

scope.surface: Agent Context team — search, retrieval, and knowledge extraction within Asana's AI/work graph platform
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, enterprise work management platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer impact of AI platform improvements
core_responsibilities: Drive the product vision and prioritized roadmap for Agent Context, tying work directly to customer impact and Asana's mission.
Improve retrieval quality, coverage, and performance across search and RAG experiences to make AI more trustworthy.
Define success metrics and use data-driven insights to guide product decisions and crisp trade-offs.
Partner closely with engineering, design, and data science to ship high-impact platform improvements.
Collaborate with GTM teams and stakeholders to ensure enterprise AI is dependable, safe, and aligned with governance standards.
Shape capabilities that enable shared organizational memory, allowing humans and agents to coordinate actions effortlessly.

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of compensation package (form not specified)

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($202K-$230K base) and competitive for NYC market
Clear product surface and ownership area (Agent Context team, search/RAG/retrieval focus)
Role tightly tied to a specific, growing AI-native product area
Yearly pay equity audit commitment mentioned, signals structured comp fairness
red_flags: NYC-only hybrid role with significant in-office days (Mon, Tue, Thu mandatory); eliminates remote candidates
No mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for international candidates
High buzzword density — 'agentic enterprise,' 'coordination and memory layer,' 'organizational memory' may indicate early-stage or aspirational product definition
Friday in-office policy is ambiguous and team-dependent

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior' and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and company context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
ambiguities: The 'ideally focusing on search, retrieval, AI, or platform products' qualifier on the 5+ years experience could be read as a nice-to-have domain focus rather than a hard requirement; treated as a qualifier on a hard requirement here.
Friday in-office policy is conditionally stated: 'depends on the type of work you do and the teams with which you partner' — not fully deterministic.
Equity form not specified (options, RSUs, etc.); only mentioned as a potential component.
